frameworks.name: spatial suppression / center-surround antagonism
frameworks.one_line: Suppressive center-surround mechanisms subtract background motion to expose moving objects.

frameworks.name: figure-ground segmentation
frameworks.one_line: Motion-based segregation reframed as a consequence of background-motion insensitivity.

frameworks.name: perceptual learning
frameworks.one_line: Training alters spatial suppression strength and concomitant segregation ability.

core_question: Does spatial suppression of background motion signals causally support rapid figure-ground segmentation of moving objects?
key_assumptions: Center-surround suppression effectively subtracts large-field background motion
Individual differences in motion processing index underlying suppression strength
Motion segregation and spatial suppression share a common neural substrate
manipulations: Individual differences in spatial suppression strength
key_findings: Spatial suppression strength predicts motion segregation ability across individuals and stimuli
Aging brings superior background-motion perception alongside impaired motion segregation
Training improves segregation only when it also reduces background-motion sensitivity
interpretations: Insensitivity to large moving stimuli implements background subtraction that drives motion segregation
Spatial suppression is a critical computational substrate for rapid motion-based figure-ground segregation
